The output mentions these figures in passing as part of broader violation summaries but never specifically addresses Bellini's individual legal exposure, his personal relationship with Ostrowski (owner of 40% of Lakeshore Trading LLC), or frames these facts as constituting Bellini's personal liability for the directed brokerage/kickback scheme. The criterion requires a summary of Bellini's personal exposure including his relationship with Ostrowski, which is only briefly mentioned once ('undisclosed personal relationship with Kevin Ostrowski, who owns 40% of the firm') without any dedicated analysis of Bellini's individual risk or sanctions. The output lacks a focused treatment of Bellini's individual exposure as a named respondent.

criteria_results.title: Total Alleged Ill-Gotten Gains / Client Harm Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states the total alleged ill-gotten gains/client harm in multiple sections. For example, in the 'Failure to Maintain Required Books and Records' section: 'Total alleged ill-gotten gains / client harm: $8,622,000' with the breakdown of '$8,238,000 cherry-picking' and '$384,000' for undisclosed commissions and compensation. The 'Triggers for Additional Reviews' section also states 'Total alleged financial harm amounts to $8,622,000' with the component breakdown of $7,800,000 (Oceanic Fund), $438,000 (Chao's account), $291,400 (excess commissions), and $92,600 (undisclosed compensation). This satisfies the criterion of providing an aggregate monetary figure of approximately $8.622 million.

criteria_results.title: Hargrove & Tilton Review: Failure to Test Trade Allocation Noted
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output references the Hargrove & Tilton compliance review multiple times (noting deficiencies in written policies, the dual-hatted compliance officer arrangement, and general compliance issues), but never specifically mentions that the H&T annual compliance review failed to test trade allocation patterns despite the firm's heavy reliance on omnibus trading. This specific omission — the failure to test trade allocation — is the key evidence of deficient compliance infrastructure identified in the criterion, and it is absent from the agent's output.

criteria_results.title: Key Dates: Examination Timeline Accurately Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not include any of the five specific examination timeline dates: examination commenced March 11, 2024; deficiency letter issued August 2, 2024; referral to Enforcement October 18, 2024; referral notice dated November 6, 2024; or served November 12, 2024. The output mentions only that the referral was made 'On October , 2024' (with the day missing) in a key figure citation, and references a December 12, 2024 response deadline. None of the five required dates are accurately stated, falling well below the minimum threshold of three correct dates required to pass.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's 12% LP Interest Value Correctly Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP multiple times, and it references the $185M figure (from wca-form-adv-part2a-march2023.docx) in a key figures citation, but it never explicitly calculates or states that the 12% interest was valued at approximately $22.2 million (12% × $185M). The output only mentions the 12% interest and the $185M figure separately without connecting them to derive the $22.2 million valuation.

criteria_results.title: Compliance Manual Last Updated November 15, 2022
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output never mentions the specific date 'November 15, 2022' as the last update date for WCA's Compliance Manual. While the output references the Compliance Manual multiple times (as 'Exhibit E' and 'wca-compliance-manual-excerpts.docx'), it does not include the specific last-updated date of November 15, 2022.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski's 40% Ownership Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states in the 'Failure to Maintain Required Books and Records' section: 'Marcus T. Bellini, a portfolio manager, directed client trades to Lakeshore Trading LLC based on his undisclosed personal relationship with Kevin Ostrowski, who owns 40% of the firm.' This correctly identifies both Kevin Ostrowski's identity and his 40% ownership stake in Lakeshore Trading LLC.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski-Bellini Personal Relationship and Undisclosed Nature
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions that Marcus T. Bellini directed client trades to Lakeshore Trading LLC based on an 'undisclosed personal relationship with Kevin Ostrowski, who owns 40% of the firm,' but it never specifies that Ostrowski was Bellini's college roommate. The criterion requires identification of the specific nature of the relationship (college roommate) as well as its undisclosed nature. While the undisclosed nature is mentioned, the specific personal relationship (college roommate) is omitted throughout the entire output.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output never references the Whitmore-to-Delgado email (whitmore-delgado-email.eml) or its specific language such as 'clean up the shared drive' or 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive.' While the output does mention that Whitmore instructed employees to delete trade allocation records during an SEC examination, it does not cite or quote the specific email exhibit or note its ambiguous language that could support an innocent interpretation.
